llm_response:
# Resignation Letter

Dear [Boss's Name],

I am writing to formally inform you that I have decided to resign from my position as [Your Job Title], with my last day of employment set for [Date]. This was not an easy decision to make, as I have truly valued my time here, but I have come to the conclusion that it is the right step for me to take at this moment. I prefer not to disclose my reasons for resigning in detail at this time, but I want to ensure that my departure is handled with the utmost professionalism and respect.

My decision to resign stems from a combination of seeking new career growth opportunities and addressing certain personal circumstances that require my full attention. While I have learned immensely during my tenure, I feel that moving forward will allow me to focus on areas where I can contribute more effectively and align better with my long-term goals. I want to express my sincere gratitude for the support you have provided, and I want to say thank you for the opportunity to work under your leadership and grow within this organization.

To ensure a smooth transition, I have outlined the following key points for my departure:
*   Completing all pending projects before my final day and documenting current workflows.
*   Training my designated colleague on my primary responsibilities to ensure continuity.
*   Returning all company property and accessing necessary files for archival purposes.

I am committed to making this transition as seamless as possible and will do everything in my power to assist during this period. Thank you for the opportunity to be part of this team, and I hope we can stay in touch in the future. Please let me know how you would like to proceed with the next steps regarding my resignation.
